pr.title: Run `cargo update` to address Dependapot alert
pr.author: ribbanya

summary: Updated the Rust dependency lockfile by running `cargo update`, apparently to resolve a Dependabot/Dependapot security alert. The PR changed only `Cargo.lock`, refreshing many crate versions and checksums used by the repository's Rust tooling without touching decompilation C/ASM source.

key_files.reason: Only changed file; records the updated resolved Rust dependency graph after `cargo update`.

what_changed: Refreshed `Cargo.lock` with 198 added lines and 300 deleted lines across 36 hunks.

Updated many Rust crates, including examples visible in the diff such as `ahash`, `aho-corasick`, `anstream`, `anstyle`, `anyhow`, `argh`, `bstr`, `clap`, `csv`, `dashmap`, `env_logger`, `errno`, `getrandom`, `globset`, `hashbrown`, `is-terminal`, `itoa`, `libc`, `lock_api`, `log`, `memchr`, `once_cell`, `parking_lot_core`, `proc-macro2`, `quote`, `rayon`, and `redox_syscall`.
Updated repository Rust tool package dependency references in the lockfile, including `melee-diff-symbols`, `melee-fix-labels`, and `melee-replace-symbols` moving from `ahash 0.8.3` to `ahash 0.8.6`.
Updated the git-locked `cwparse` source revision from `d0371e91e14f1e663dfdc1fff1db76afe3e6035e` to `6b0b406b13af5c43cc24dd26ff0f078e5fa15be3`.

Removed or collapsed some older transitive dependency entries in the lockfile, such as `cc`, `crossbeam-channel`, `errno-dragonfly`, an older `hermit-abi`, `io-lifetimes`, and `num_cpus`, as a consequence of the dependency graph refresh.
Normalized some lockfile dependency names after updates, for example references like `windows-sys 0.48.0` became `windows-sys` in the updated graph.
smart_moves: Kept the fix scoped to `Cargo.lock`, which is appropriate for a dependency resolution/security-alert update when no source changes are required.
Used the standard Cargo-generated lockfile update path instead of manually editing dependency versions, preserving resolver consistency and checksums.
Allowed transitive dependency cleanup to fall out of the resolver update, reducing duplicate or obsolete lockfile entries where upstream crates no longer needed them.
decomp_lessons: Security and dependency maintenance for Rust helper tools can be isolated from decompilation matching work by touching only `Cargo.lock` when source compatibility is unchanged.
For future tooling PRs, lockfile-only updates are easy to review when the PR title clearly states that `cargo update` was run and the changed-file set is limited to the lockfile.
This PR contains no evidence of new decompilation matching tactics, symbol naming decisions, struct layout work, or C/ASM changes.
naming_conventions: No project symbol or decompilation naming conventions were introduced or discussed.
The PR title uses the term `Dependapot`, likely referring to a Dependabot alert, but there is no body or review discussion clarifying the spelling.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: None; the PR only updated Rust dependency resolution in `Cargo.lock` and did not modify assembly, C source, matching code, or build-output comparison logic.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review records were present in the provided PR slice.
follow_up_queries: Cargo.lock cargo update dependency security alert
